DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF DANIEL OCHOA'S MOTION FOR ORDERS COMPELLING FURTNER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND GRANT SANCTIONS AGAINST T-MOBILE IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 13, 2020 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT 301. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE IS A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND MOTION TO STAY THAT WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE SCHULMAN ON OCTOBER 19, 2020. IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED, THIS DISCOVERY MOTION SHALL BE REFERRED TO THE ARBITRATOR AND WILL BE ORDERED OFF CALENDAR IN DEPT 301. IF THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED, THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION WILL GO FORWARD ON NOVEMBER 13, 2020 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT 301. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE AN OPPOSITION BY OCTOBER 30, 2020 AND PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE A REPLY BY NOVEMBER 6, 2020. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Dec-02-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay All Litigation Between Assurant, Inc. And Daniel Ochoa; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ASSURANT INC.'s Petition to Compel Arbitration and Stay All Litigation Between Assurant, Inc. and Daniel Ochoa.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court indicates it received a notice of contest from plaintiff by email who indicated to the Court an inability to appear this morning via Zoom. The Court indicates it provided the plaintiff with instructions by reply email as to how to appear in person. The Court confirms with the clerk that plaintiff has not appeared in person at the courthouse this morning. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff apparently served opposition on defendants, but no opposition was filed with the court. Although plaintiff is representing himself, that does not relieve him from the obligation to follow the rules. (See, e.g., ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208 [self-represented litigants must follow correct rules of procedure].) Plaintiff, who filed a discovery motion and accompanying papers on September 30, knows how to file papers with the court. In any event, the court has reviewed plaintiff's combined opposition papers, which are attached to defendant's reply, and they lack merit.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder In T-Mobile Usa, Inc.S Motion To Compel Arbitration

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA INC's Motion to Compel Arbitration and to Stay Action by Defendant T-Mobile USA, Inc.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court indicates it received a notice of contest from plaintiff by email who indicated to the Court an inability to appear this morning via Zoom. The Court indicates it provided the plaintiff with instructions by reply email as to how to appear in person. The Court confirms with the clerk that plaintiff has not appeared in person at the courthouse this morning. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Motion granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff apparently served opposition on defendants, but no opposition was filed with the court. Although plaintiff is representing himself, that does not relieve him from the obligation to follow the rules. (See, e.g., ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208 [self-represented litigants must follow correct rules of procedure].) Plaintiff, who filed a discovery motion and accompanying papers on September 30, knows how to file papers with the court. In any event, the court has reviewed plaintiff's combined opposition papers, which are attached to defendant's reply, and they lack merit.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Orders Compelling Furtner Responses To Discovery And Grant Sanctions Against T-Mobile
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Continued To Nov-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL OCHOA's Motion For Orders Compelling Furtner Responses To Discovery And Grant Sanctions Against T-Mobile

Continued to November 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 301. The Court notes that there is a motion to compel arbitration and motion to stay that will be heard by Judge Schulman on October 19, 2020. If the motion is granted, this discovery motion shall be referred to the arbitrator and will be ordered off calendar in Dept 301. If the motion to compel arbitration is denied, the hearing on this motion will go forward on November 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 301. Defendants shall file an opposition by October 30, 2020 and Plaintiff shall file a reply by November 6, 2020.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
